meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster.
content.answer_block: The lack of version control is a ticking time bomb for freelancers. By adopting a pre-configured SaaS starter kit, you integrate Git workflows, automated deployments, and environment management by default. Using tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can initialize production-ready environments in minutes, ensuring every line of code is tracked.
content.intro:
You’re three weeks into a client project, the features are piling up, and suddenly—a critical bug emerges. You reach for your history, but there is none. Just a folder of timestamped zip files named 'final_v2_fixed'. The anxiety of breaking a production app without a safety net isn't just unprofessional; it’s a direct threat to your freelance income.

But what if you could bypass the 'cowboy coding' phase entirely? By leveraging a modern SaaS starter kit, you shift from manual file-swapping to a robust, version-controlled architecture. This isn't just about saving code; it’s about scaling your freelance business with the speed and reliability of a senior engineering team.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Repository
steps.description: Initialize your project with a SaaS starter kit that enforces Git best practices from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new SaaS project structure using the standard starter kit template, ensuring Git is pre-configured with a .gitignore and branch protection rules.

steps.title: Automate Branching Strategy
steps.description: Establish a clear workflow for features versus fixes to ensure your main branch stays stable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a GitHub Flow branching strategy that requires pull request reviews before merging into the main production branch.

steps.title: Integrate Environment Variables
steps.description: Securely manage your API keys and secrets using encrypted environment files to prevent accidental leakage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a secure environment variable management system using dotenv and ensure all .env files are properly git-ignored.

steps.title: Implement Continuous Deployment
steps.description: Automate your deployment pipeline so every merge to main triggers an instant, tested production build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a CI/CD pipeline configuration file for GitHub Actions that runs tests and deploys to Vercel on every push to main.

steps.title: Audit and Sync
steps.description: Run a final audit to ensure no local changes are left untracked before handing over the project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a repository audit to ensure all files are tracked and provide a summary of the current git status.

pro_tips: Always use 'git commit -m' with descriptive prefixes like 'feat:', 'fix:', or 'chore:' to make your history readable for future you.
Never manually edit production code; if it isn't in a feature branch, it doesn't exist.
Use Claude Code to generate 'conventional commits' automatically based on your changes to keep your log professional.
Set up a 'post-merge' hook that automatically runs your test suite, so you never deploy broken code.
Keep your starter kit updated by pulling from the upstream repository weekly to benefit from security patches.
content.next_steps: After establishing your repository, perform a dry-run deployment to a staging environment to verify your pipeline before pushing to production.
content.conversion_block: Stop losing sleep over lost code and start shipping like a pro. With the right starter kit, you can reclaim your time and double your output. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
